title: mRemoteNG.appSettings - Consider moving to AppData Roaming
author: david-dell
body_snippet:
**Issue**

Unable to launch mRemoteNG if build is copied to C:\Program Files\..... and then running as a user that does not have **write** access to said folder. Issue appears to be due to the applied wanting to write to **mRemoteNG.appSettings** within the same folder as the executable.

**Solution (Possible)?**

Consider moving the **mRemoteNG.appSettings** file so it is loaded from AppData Roaming (similar to connection files), rather than within the same folder as the executable.

https://gi...

comments.author: david-dell
comments.snippet:
Hey @Kvarkas 

Scenario I'm looking at (and suspect this is common), is a folder with the mR binaries that would be read only (as users shouldn't be changing the binaries) for simultaneous access (on the same system - multi-session host) for 20 to 30 people.

As the folder is read only, the database file, like you said is unable to create/write the db file.

With that in mind, the question I have is:

1. Is the DB expected to support multiple users accessing the same file at the same time?

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

**Commit:** `5dc3bad0` (fork `robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`)
**What changed:** Change the settings file base path from AppDomain.CurrentDom

A beta build (v1.81.0-beta.4) including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Ou...

notes:
AI triage (claude): Moving appSettings to AppData Roaming follows Windows best practices for per-user config and fixes multi-session/read-only binary folder scenarios where 20-30 users share the same install path.
Approach: Change the settings file base path from AppDomain.CurrentDomain.BaseDirectory to Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.ApplicationData) + "\mRemoteNG\", with migration logic for existing installations
